heading: Grants to States
label: 672 Grants to States
notes: Change of Name“Secretary of Health and Human Services” substituted for “Secretary of Health, Education, and Welfare” in subsec. (c), pursuant to which is classified to , Education.section 509(b) of Pub. L. 96–88section 3508(b) of Title 20

children.heading: Designation of State agency to assist State in identifying State needs and responsibilities and in developing State plans
children.label: a Designation of State agency to assist State in identifying State needs and responsibilities and in developing State plans

children.text: in identifying their needs and responsibilities in the area of occupational safety and health,
in developing State plans under , orsection 667 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: establishing systems for the collection of information concerning the nature and frequency of occupational injuries and diseases;

children.label: B
children.text: increasing the expertise and enforcement capabilities of their personnel engaged in occupational safety and health programs; or

children.label: C
children.text: otherwise improving the administration and enforcement of State occupational safety and health laws, including standards thereunder, consistent with the objectives of this chapter.

children.heading: Experimental and demonstration projects
children.label: b Experimental and demonstration projects
children.text: The Secretary is authorized, during the fiscal year ending , and the two succeeding fiscal years, to make grants to the States for experimental and demonstration projects consistent with the objectives set forth in subsection (a) of this section.June 30, 1971

children.heading: Designation by Governor of appropriate State agency for receipt of grant
children.label: c Designation by Governor of appropriate State agency for receipt of grant
children.text: The Governor of the State shall designate the appropriate State agency for receipt of any grant made by the Secretary under this section.

children.heading: Submission of application
children.label: d Submission of application
children.text: Any State agency designated by the Governor of the State desiring a grant under this section shall submit an application therefor to the Secretary.

children.heading: Approval or rejection of application
children.label: e Approval or rejection of application
children.text: The Secretary shall review the application, and shall, after consultation with the Secretary of Health and Human Services, approve or reject such application.

children.heading: Federal share
children.label: f Federal share
children.text: The Federal share for each State grant under subsection (a) or (b) of this section may not exceed 90 per centum of the total cost of the application. In the event the Federal share for all States under either such subsection is not the same, the differences among the States shall be established on the basis of objective criteria.

children.heading: Administration and enforcement of programs contained in approved State plans; Federal share
children.label: g Administration and enforcement of programs contained in approved State plans; Federal share
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to make grants to the States to assist them in administering and enforcing programs for occupational safety and health contained in State plans approved by the Secretary pursuant to . The Federal share for each State grant under this subsection may not exceed 50 per centum of the total cost to the State of such a program. The last sentence of subsection (f) shall be applicable in determining the Federal share under this subsection.section 667 of this title

children.heading: Report to President and Congress
children.label: h Report to President and Congress
children.text: Prior to , the Secretary shall, after consultation with the Secretary of Health and Human Services, transmit a report to the President and to the Congress, describing the experience under the grant programs authorized by this section and making any recommendations he may deem appropriate.June 30, 1973
